name: Web Components
description: Building framework-agnostic, reusable UI components using Web Components standards
definition: Web Components are a set of web platform APIs that allow you to create reusable custom elements with encapsulated functionality

custom_elements.example: class MyButton extends HTMLElement { constructor() { super(); } }

lifecycle.connectedCallback: Called when element added to DOM
lifecycle.adoptedCallback: Called when moved to new document

shadow_dom.purpose: Styles don't leak in or out, true encapsulation

html_templates.usage: Define HTML structure that can be cloned

benefits.framework_agnostic: Works with React, Vue, Angular, vanilla JS
benefits.encapsulation: Styles and DOM scoped, no conflicts
benefits.future_proof: Web standard, won't be deprecated like frameworks

limitations.ssr: Server-side rendering is complex (Declarative Shadow DOM helps)
limitations.seo: Search engines may not index shadow DOM content well
limitations.react_compat: React has historical issues with Web Components (improving)

css_custom_properties.example:
:host { background: var(--button-bg, blue); }
/* User can set: my-button { --button-bg: red; } */

events.example:
this.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('change', { detail: value }));
/* User: element.addEventListener('change', e => console.log(e.detail)); */

attributes_vs_properties.attributes: Strings only, reflect in HTML, use for configuration
attributes_vs_properties.properties: Any type, JavaScript only, use for complex data
attributes_vs_properties.sync: Reflect important properties to attributes for SSR/SEO

accessibility.aria: Use ARIA roles and properties (role, aria-label, etc.)
accessibility.keyboard: Implement keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Arrow keys)

best_practices: Expose styling via CSS custom properties
Use Lit or Stencil for better DX
Support SSR if needed (Declarative Shadow DOM)
Use CSS Parts for deep styling access (::part)
anti_patterns: Not using Shadow DOM (loses encapsulation benefit)
